output.code:
# Load the file to inspect
with open('/mnt/data/file-FwHGARANdnZ992nZLyK2yT-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
text[:500]

# find positions of the quote marks for hadith start and maybe end
start_index = text.find('قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم:')
start_index, text[start_index:start_index+50]

# find the start of the quote
quote_start = text.find('"', start_index)
quote_start

# find the next closing quote after this
quote_end = text.find('"', quote_start+1)
quote_end

# We'll find spans programmatically.
text = text  # already loaded

# Span 1: first ayah
span1 = 'إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْغَاوِينَ'
start1 = text.find(span1)
end1 = start1 + len(span1)
print('span1 start,end:', start1, end1, text[start1:end1] == span1)

# Span 2: second ayah
span2 = 'إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لَهُ سُلْطَانٌ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَلَى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ'
start2 = text.find(span2)
end2 = start2 + len(span2)
print('span2 start,end:', start2, end2, text[start2:end2] == span2)

# Hadith
narr = 'قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم:'
n_idx = text.find(narr)
q_idx = text.find('"', n_idx)
span3 = text[q_idx+1: len(text)]
# Let's trim leading/trailing whitespace, quotes, punctuation? 
# Remove leading/trailing spaces and newlines:
span3 = span3.strip()
# But also maybe leading/trailing quotes/punct, but leading is first char is Arabic letter: ا
# So check:
start3 = text.find(span3)
end3 = start3 + len(span3)
print('span3 start,end:', start3, end3, text[start3:end3] == span3)
print('First 50 of span3:', span3[:50])
print('Last 50 of span3:', span3[-50:])
